$comment: VENDORED COPY for the WS-A contract test in claude-copilot/tools/cc. Source of truth is copilot-control-tower/docs/01-architecture/schemas/deprovision.schema.json — update this copy from there, not the reverse.
title: copilot deprovision <org> --json
description: Result of removing an org's materialized tree and read-only clones. `secrets_touched` MUST be 0 (no secret ever lived in a layer). Dirty trees are retained, never destroyed.

result.$comment: CORRECTED 2026-07-07: the upstream design gives an explicit enum (design-control-tower-integration.md §1.4): `{ result: "wiped"|"partial"|"noop", ... }`. The prior draft left this as an unconstrained non-empty string.

materialized.$comment: CORRECTED 2026-07-07: the upstream design's example (design-control-tower-integration.md §1.4) is `removed: { materialized: N, clones: [layer…] }` -- `N` is a count, not a boolean. The prior draft encoded this as a boolean ('was the tree removed'); changed to a non-negative integer to match. `deprovision.removed.materialized`'s exact semantics (count of what -- files? dimensions?) remain genuinely unspecified even in the upstream design; this is an open field to be defined at freeze, per the CLI owner.

retained_dirty.description: Dirty/human-owned working trees that were preserved, never destroyed (invariant #3).

secrets_touched.description: Count of secrets touched. MUST be 0.
secrets_touched.$comment: cli-contract.md states secrets_touched MUST be 0 (no secret ever lived in a layer). Encoded as const:0 so any nonzero value is a hard schema violation caught by the CI contract test.
